rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LILLIE VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Deft J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc'S Mo To Dismiss, Or In The Alternative, Mo To Continue Trial; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Certainteed Corporation's joinder in J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s motion to dismiss is granted. Certainteed provided proper notice of its joinder and no opposition was filed. Certainteed Corp. is dismissed from this action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LILLIE VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Joinder To J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Continue Trial With Proof Of Service

Defendant Grinnell LLC's joinder in J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s motion to dismiss, or in the alternative, motion to continue trial date is off calendar at Grinnell's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE KASE VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint

If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
